channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics for tumor microenvironment modulation in solid tumor therapy
item.description: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics represent an emerging approach for modulating the tumor microenvironment in solid tumor treatment. These nanotechnology-based therapeutics are designed to target and alter the cellular and molecular conditions surrounding tumors, potentially improving therapeutic eff

item.title: Solo-maxxing: how tech taught gen z to monetise being alone
item.description: We can no longer pretend that the bridge between generations is invisible, because it stands before us, growing wider every day, harder to cross and harder even to name. Nor can we deny that technology has had its hand in this rupture, since it has not merely…

item.title: How atlantic health cut legal document search time by 42% with amazon bedrock metadata filtering
item.description: In this post, you learn how to implement metadata-filtered RAG for multi-entity document search using Amazon Bedrock Agents and Amazon OpenSearch Service.

item.title: Metabolic determinants of cancer immunotherapy outcomes identified by plasma profiling
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based metabolomic analysis of plasma samples from multiple cohorts of patients treated with immunotherapy across five distinct tumor types, followed by machine learning enabled identification of metabolic signatures, as well as functional ex…

item.title: Advances and challenges of splicing prediction with AI
item.description: Predicting splicing outcomes is a central challenge in precision medicine. This Review traces the evolution of computational approaches from early statistical heuristics to modern artificial intelligence frameworks to characterize splicing events.

item.title: The cpu's growing role in agentic AI infrastructure
item.description: PARTNER CONTENT: As agentic AI systems scale across cloud and datacenter environments, CPUs remain the control plane coordinating performance and efficiency.

item.title: Divergent effects of pathological α-synuclein truncations and mutations on phase separation
item.description: This study investigates how different pathological forms of α-synuclein—specifically truncations and mutations—produce divergent effects on phase separation, a process implicated in protein aggregation relevant to neurodegenerative disease. The research characterizes the biophysical behavior of thes

item.title: STAT+: FDA’s breakthrough pipeline fills up with generative AI devices
item.description: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: FDA drops enforcement against Whoop, more AI devices get FDA 'breakthrough' label, and more.

item.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
item.description: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

item.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
item.description: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
item.description: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

item.title: Data-driven surrogates of rational design enable antimicrobial peptide optimization
item.description: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 25 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01258-0 Rising pathogen drug resistance makes next-generation antimicrobial peptides a global priority. Generative AI accelerates discovery by rapidly proposing new peptides with high therapeutic potential. The key q

item.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
item.description: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.

item.title: Image-guided activation of drugs with electromagnetic radiation
item.description: Image-guided pharmacotherapy holds great promise for personalized medicine through confining drug activity precisely to the disease area using electromagnetic irradiation. This Perspective describes the molecular principles behind drug activation using electr…

item.title: Zero-shot design of drug-binding proteins via neural iterative selection−expansion
item.description: By pairing two neural networks in an iterative optimization algorithm, small-molecule binding proteins can be designed from scratch with high accuracy, affinity and success rates, showing promise for applications in drug delivery and sequestra…

item.title: New york democrat micah lasher wins house primary that drew big spending from AI groups
item.description: New York Assemblyman Micah Lasher has won the Democratic nomination in the 12th Congressional District, NBC News projects, benefitting from his deep ties to the area and backing from party leaders in a primary that garnered national attention.

item.title: Canada's AI strategy aims to make health data more accessible. how will it protect privacy?
item.description: Connecting health data across the country will help researchers conduct clinical trials, test AI health tools and drive innovation in treatment and diagnosis, says federal Minister of Artificial Intelligence Evan Solomon. But the initiative will also have to …

item.title: Show hn: sql mcp server – 61.37% on dataagentbench with glm-5.2
item.description: We just posted results to the DataAgentBench leaderboard scoring 61.37% with GLM 5.2. Please check it out and do share your feedback Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48656497 Points: 1 # Comments: 0

item.title: What is the betterhelp scandal? is betterhelp legit? everything you've ever wanted to know about the online therapy giant
item.description: BetterHelp has grown into the world's largest online therapy platform by doing something that the traditional mental health care system has long struggled to accomplish: making licensed therapy accessible to people who might otherwise never pursue it.

item.title: Digital sovereignty is no longer optional - agentic AI has made it fundamental
item.description: How business can prepare for Agentic AI.

item.title: Dietary cholesterol activates a ral-dependent pathway driving ldlr turnover
item.description: Chronic dietary cholesterol activates Ral GTPases, which promote LDLR internalization and lysosomal degradation through RalBP1–REPS1 and CTSA, thereby reducing cholesterol clearance, whereas CTSA inhibition restores LDLR function and may offer a therapeutic s…

item.title: Fusobacterium periodonticum promotes colorectal tumorigenesis via decanoic acid-driven neutrophil chemotaxis
item.description: Here, using a multi-omics approach, the authors reveal that Fusobacterium periodonticum is enriched in colorectal cancer and correlates with elevated decanoic acid, driving neutrophil chemotaxis via a G-protein-dependent mechanism to promote colorectal tumori…

item.title: Disparate privacy risks from medical AI
item.description: AI models for medical diagnostics are vulnerable to membership inference attacks.

item.title: Facilitating structure-based drug discovery with an artificial intelligence-driven virtual screening platform
item.description: This protocol describes an artificial intelligence-driven virtual screening platform designed to facilitate structure-based drug discovery. The platform integrates AI methods to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of identifying promising drug candidates from large molecular databases through comput

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

item.title: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
item.description: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new…

item.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
item.description: The silicon race is heating up amid the struggle to keep up with demand.

item.title: Why investors are finally separating quantum computing winners from losers
item.description: For much of the past two years, investors treated quantum computing as a single investment theme. Whether a company pursued trapped-ion systems, superconduct...

item.title: Predictive value of pfemp1 antibody profiles for the course of controlled human malaria infections
item.description: Author summary Malaria remains a major global health burden, and effective control depends on a detailed understanding of host immune responses to Plasmodium falciparum. In this study, we investigated antibody responses against P. falciparum erythrocyte membr…

item.title: The AI industry just won a race — but lost the war
item.description: New York’s congressional primaries on Tuesday were supposed to be the moment AI’s top backers definitively proved they could bend US politics to their will. Instead, they left behind a muddy stalemate that only raises more questions about whether their spendi…

item.title: The age of stupid—and self-serving—ai regulation is here!
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI may not like current federal controls on their products, but it will be consumers who end up getting screwed.

item.title: AI super pac’s first major target loses new york congressional primary
item.description: The PAC backed by OpenAI's president won it's fight against the candidate, but it didn't win the whole race.

item.title: The $27 million al proxy war over alex bores ends in a draw
item.description: The expensive, $27 million political proxy war between Anthropic and OpenAI came to a draw last night when Alex Bores, a New York state Assemblyman whose popularity surged after being targeted by a pro-AI super PAC, narrowly lost the Democratic primary to rep…

item.title: Dems celebrate housing bill: “we finally did something”
item.description: The bipartisan housing bill the House and Senate passed this week doesn’t go far enough, Democratic leaders say. But it’s still one of the most significant pieces of housing legislation passed in decades. Further Democratic goals on housing might have to wait…

item.title: Why enterprises need to rethink data in the AI era
item.description: If data is the new oil, why are we worrying about the same old problems?

item.title: Canada puts $100m behind national health data network
item.description: VITAL will connect de-identified clinical data from 160 Canadian hospitals, giving health AI developers a national foundation to build on The post Canada puts $100M behind national health data network appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: Family history of diabetes and glycemic progression: a propensity score-based analysis using health checkup data
item.description: Background Family history of diabetes mellitus (FH_ DM) is a well-established risk factor for diabetes, but most studies have focused on disease incidence rather than glycemic changes over time. Understanding how FH_ DM affects the magnitude of change in bloo…

item.title: Fedi-code: a federated and causally-informed framework for dementia risk prediction using multi-site patient data
item.description: Early detection of dementia is critical for timely intervention and disease management, yet it remains a challenging task due to the fragmented nature of healthcare data and the need for privacy-preserving solutions. This paper proposes FEDI-CODE, a Federated…

item.title: How digital evidence and connected devices are transforming the legal profession
item.description: The legal profession has always depended on information. For generations, that information was stored in filing cabinets, paper records, handwritten notes, and physical archives. Today, however, the volume and complexity of information generated by everyday l…

item.title: How loka built a natural, low-latency voice agent with amazon nova 2 sonic
item.description: In this post, we demonstrate the architecture and approach Loka used to solve a common frustration: robotic, slow voice assistants that cause customers to hang up, damaging brand reputation and driving up support costs.

item.title: Fca boss warns AI is moving faster than the law
item.description: A top U.K. finance watchdog says regulations cannot keep pace with artificial intelligence (AI). In a speech Wednesday (June 24), Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) CEO Nikhil Rathi said regulation must therefore evolve to meet the demands of artificial intell…

item.title: New plan to make english farming profitable, productive, sustainable and resilient
item.description: The 25-year Farming Roadmap gives English farmers certainty beyond the next harvest for the first time.

item.title: Variable selection-combined causal mediation analysis for continuous treatments with application to large-dimensional biomedical data
item.description: Author summary Disease development and progress are well recognized to be influenced by multiple factors, and exploring the causal mediation effects of the mediator in the exposure-outcome association can help reveal the etiological mechanisms. Due to the wid…

item.title: Non-typhoidal salmonella co-infect and complsicate plasmodium falciparum malaria in children under-five: a prospective cohort study on clinical presentation and outcome in kisantu district hospital, dr congo
item.description: Author summary We compared non-typhoidal Salmonella (NTS) bloodstream infections and severe Plasmodium falciparum (Pf) malaria in children under five years in a high malaria transmission area in DR Congo. Among 2682 children hospitalized severe febrile illnes…

item.title: Former infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the it services world | techcrunch
item.description: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they're the most resilient | techcrunch
item.description: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave google for its rivals | techcrunch
item.description: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

item.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this u.s. company | techcrunch
item.description: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

item.title: A24 knows you’re mad about the google AI collab
item.description: Indie movie fans are upset about Google DeepMind's $75 million investment in the studio, which comes as AI companies are deepening their influence in Hollywood.

item.title: Loop engineering, latest AI buzzword, still needs humans in the loop
item.description: Prompting less and automating more comes with a price
